Comments: Productivity Project
Problem Statement:
CE EU Routing Agent - Currently, all customer communication goes through SFDC, the emails coming in need to be routed correctly. For this labels are used. However, the current process is error prone and requires high manual intervention. ​
CE EU RAG Salesforce - Currently, the standard workflow of our CE agents includes opening, reading and understanding a customer email followed by investigation in various systems (Oracle (most cases), myDSO, PowerBI, SFDC). 
Automated Summary and Response Generation - Responding to customers takes a lot of time and finding the right tone and adding all relevant information is a task that requires skill, training and time. We want to aid the agents by automated draft responses. The same goes for summarizing multiple emails and related cases.
Description:
CE EU Routing Agent - To enable Salesforce.com to intelligently analyze the content of flagged emails, determine their intent, and route them to the appropriate Salesforce queue based on context, attachment content, and / or email history. ​
CE EU RAG Salesforce -
To enable customer experience colleagues to efficiently access and retrieve data from multiple external systems - such as Oracle ERP, MyDSO credit management system, and internal product databases - directly within the Salesforce.com interface, thereby providing accurate and timely
responses to customer inquiries.​
Automated Summary and Response Generation - 
To aid our CE agents the AI should draft responses to customer queries and supervise cases within SFDC. This should allow us to be faster in our responses and aid the CE colleagues in focusing on the content more than email generation
Data Scope: SFDC, Oracle (API or Azure), MyDSO, Pbi, Labeling Table
AI Solution or Tools of Interest:
CE EU Routing Agent - Generative AI to read emails (incl. the history), open and read attachments and match the content with the provided routing logic. Agentic AI and / Or SFDC integration to create cases and provide summaries. 
CE EU RAG Salesforce - Generative AI to read emails (incl. the history), open and read attachments and understand the query / task / question. Then it should translate that into a technical query and retrieve relevant information from the respective system / DB and display it to an agent
Automated Summary and Response Generation - Based on the content of the customer emails and the retrieved information (use case 2), the Gen-AI model should draft a response, matching the right tone and including the relevant details before the CE agent can validate and submit. He should be able to interact with it and adjust the content.

Use Case: Project Clever - AI for Clinical Docs (Duplicated)
Description: Automating Clinical Evaluation Reports (CERs) offers a valuable solution for addressing the backlog of FDA-approved devices that haven't entered other markets due to missing CERs. By automating this process,Stryker divisions can accelerate market entry, generate revenue, and significantly reduce operating costs. With CERs typically costing $100K per report, and dozens of devices in the backlog, the potential savings are substantial. Automation also ensures that CERs are regularly updated according to regulatory requirements—particularly important for high-risk devices needing annual updates and low-risk devices requiring updates every 2-5 years. This approach helps devices reach new markets faster, benefiting both the company and patients.

Description:
Below are some basic system requirements that FlexiCapture currently offers us:

•The system shall be capable of scanning between 500 to 800 pages per day and support batch scanning of up to 200 pages at once.
•The system shall automatically parse, and group scanned pages into documents based on predefined label recognition rules (at least 5 different known label types, but system is capable of recognizing new labels for future changes) (see attached example DHR, current FlexiCapture system recognizes this label as a new document when scanning in large batches).
•The system shall recognize and differentiate between several predefined product label formats using pattern recognition, machine learning, or AI (see attached 2 different labels where required fields are in different locations on the label)
•The system shall extract the following fields from each recognized label:
- Product number
- Serial number
- Date of manufacture (in format YYYY-MM-DD)
•Extracted data shall be validated against field-specific rules (e.g., serial number must be alphanumeric, product numbers must match a reference table).
•The system shall convert scanned documents into PDF format and upload them to a cloud-based storage system.
• The system shall expose extracted label data and scan data through a database view accessible to a connected Power BI dashboard. (link)
To build a process around AI Governance

Description:
INSTRUMENTS
By leveraging AI to revolutionize our order entry process, we are poised to deliver substantial operational and strategic value to our call center. The deployment of automated email review and entry solutions will eliminate up to 90% of manual email order handling, enabling agents to focus on more complex, customer-centric tasks. This targeted automation will streamline workflows, improve service delivery, and significantly elevate both productivity and employee morale.
Currently, the INST call center dedicates over 2,200 minutes each day to processing approximately 275 email orders. Through automation, this effort can be reduced by an estimated 1,980 minutes daily, resulting in a weekly savings of 165 hours, or 8,580 hours annually. The reclamation of these hours offers a strategic advantage: enhancing workforce capacity, supporting future scalability, and strengthening our commitment to exceptional customer service. This initiative directly aligns with our core business objectives of operational efficiency, sustainable growth, and service excellence.
Furthermore, the highly manual nature of the current order entry process often results in errors, with an accuracy rate of 89.33%. Our goal is to achieve world-class standards by improving accuracy to 99%. Addressing these inefficiencies will minimize duplicate orders, incorrect shipments, and customer credits—ultimately fostering greater customer satisfaction and boosting the confidence of our sales teams.
COMM, ENDO and SPORTS MED are also interested in this functionality.
Success Criteria:
Improve first time quality of email orders to 99%
Reduce processing time of orders by 30%.  
Process email orders 7x24 as opposed to M-F business hours.
Reduce the number of customer credits, duplicate orders, etc. over the long term.
Overall, increase satisfaction among customers and salespeople – NPS Score.
Data Source & Usage:
The data that is validated and reviewed in this process include customer name, ship to address, account number, product number, price (contract and list), type of order (bill only, no charge, capital, etc.), delivery date, delivery method, etc.  The sources of this data include the customer purchase order, Salesforce, Model N, SAP, CPQ, etc.

Description:
Executive Summary (Use Case Overview): 
The ProCare Business Report Card initiative aims to transform how customer account performance and service interactions are evaluated and communicated. Currently, compiling these statistics is a manual, time-intensive process that can hinder timely insights and strategic decision-making. By introducing an automated reporting system that draws data from platforms like SAP, ServiceMax, and Salesforce, Stryker seeks to streamline reporting, enhance the quality of account analyses, and support the ProCare Team in identifying trends, addressing recurring service issues, and uncovering new business opportunities.
The automated reports will offer clear, visually appealing summaries of service history, response times, and contract performance, empowering teams to make more informed decisions and proactively manage customer relationships. This initiative is expected to improve customer satisfaction through enhanced transparency, increased efficiency, and better support for sales, renewals, and strategic planning—ultimately enabling Stryker to deliver superior customer care and drive business growth.
Strategic Alignment to Our Business Objectives:
The initiative aligns the Instruments Strategy and Business Objectives to streamline sales requests and interactions leading to increased quality and efficiency and to scale for future growth.
Business Case
Value
INSTRUMENTS
Today, it is a very manual process to pull account performance statistics to evaluate the viability of a customer and to provide a customer with the reason to purchase or renew their ProCare contract.  By providing a streamlined systematic way to present the data it will be easier for the ProCare Team to draw insights into the companies that can impact pricing and account strategies.  It will also assist the Sales Teams to upselling and cross new equipment.  The reports may be utilized in the selling cycle as well as in Quarterly Business Reviews.
The value these Report Cards bring are vast.  The benefits range from helping with managing the renewal process, maintaining a continuous focus on the customer, increasing brand recognition, impact pricing, and increasing the number of the renewals. 
The current process of compiling account performance statistics and summarizing customer service interactions is highly manual and time-consuming. This often limits the ProCare Team’s ability to quickly identify key trends or address service issues in a timely manner. By developing an automated reporting solution that generates professional, visually appealing presentations using data sourced from systems such as SAP, ServiceMax, and Salesforce, Stryker can transform how customer service data is leveraged.
Automated reports would provide clear, concise summaries of the services customers receive, highlighting important metrics such as service history, response times, and contract performance. These insights would empower the ProCare Team to proactively spot trends, identify recurring issues, and recognize opportunities for improvement or upselling. Moreover, having timely and accurate presentations at their fingertips would support sales efforts, account reviews, and strategic planning, all while reducing manual administrative workload.
The value of this initiative includes enhanced customer satisfaction through improved service transparency, more informed account management decisions, and increased operational efficiency. Ultimately, this automated reporting and presentation system would position Stryker and the ProCare Team to deliver a higher standard of customer care while supporting business growth.
To put this type of report together today, it would require multiple data sources and multiple resources and would often take days to compile this data. We would likely need to build out an entire team to be able to pull and maintain these types of reports today which is not realistic. We are also only creating these types of reports for priority customers, and it is an ad-hoc process. By being able to quickly and accurately compile these reports for any customers with the minimal amount of time and resources required, we will increase our renewal rate and customer retention and should also be able to better sell the value of ProCare to new clients. All in all, this should help to both retain and grow our business with both large and small customers. This allows us to have a consistent level of care for each customer regardless of their contract size.

Key Metrics:
1.	ROI – Repair Service Report (ProCare Value):
a.	Intended as a near-automated ROI model, potentially a spin-off of the Contract Report.
b.	All relevant data already exists within the current Contract Report.
c.	Logic is mapped in Excel; collaboration with Sam Wiedmayer planned.
d.	Example: $200K under contract vs. $300K in service value generated goes into ROI calculation.
2.	PO Customer Reporting:
a.	Consider building ROI reports for PO-based customers as well.
b.	Show what they’re currently paying and the value/opportunity available through ProCare.
3.	On-Time Completion Rate:
a.	Measure expected vs. actual service dates, including paperwork submission.
b.	For PMs: service must occur within 30 days.
c.	For Power contracts: e.g., 6 visits planned, how many completed on time?
d.	Next Step: Follow-up call required. Josh is sending related On Time metric reports.
4.	Depot Repair Turnaround Time:
 a. Track same-day shipments and advanced exchanges/loaners.
 b. Measure time taken to complete actual repair.
5.	Visit Completion Metrics:
a.	Track eligible vs. completed visits (e.g., 3 eligible visits over 18 months — were they all completed?).
6.	Equipment Uptime:
a.	Measure percentage of time equipment was operational and in which state.
7.	Pricing Metrics:
a.	Evaluate pricing in relation to customer contract value and service levels.
b.	Consider variance in pricing between multimillion-dollar contracts with floor pricing vs. smaller ($100K) contracts.
c.	Explore coverage percentage of equipment via ProCare vs. transactional spending.
8.	Proactive vs. Reactive Visits:
a.	Measure how many issues were discovered during proactive PM visits.
9.	Customer devices failure rates vs. average
a.	Shows how well customers are taking care of their equipment
b.	May allow for AI insights into best practices for reducing failures
10.	Incorporate SEM data
a.	Show how balanced their device usage is across the fleet
11.	Customer Loaner performance 
a.	Helps make the scorecard representative of both parties performance
b.	Are there outstanding delinquent loaners that we could draw attention to?
c.	Is there an average day of return we can share?
12.	Uptime cost savings
a.	Calculation based on the uptime of having a ProCare agreement and the same-day shipments delivered vs. standard repair lead time



Success Criteria:
Being able to generate a professional looking presentation with compelling business information about the ProCare Account, including:
•	Accurate data
Data Source & Usage:
The data utilized for these key performance indicators would be sources from multiple systems including SAP, ServiceMax, Salesforce, etc.
The calculations would include:
•	ROI on the overall Account
•	Number of ProCare Visits
•	Profitability of the account

Description:
Business Case
The ProCare Contract Management Team is currently challenged by the increasingly complex and high-volume task of reviewing and entering contracts manually. With the business experiencing consistent double-digit annual growth, manual processes are becoming a bottleneck, introducing risks of incomplete or inaccurate data entry. These errors can significantly disrupt downstream operations including service validation, invoicing, and ultimately the customer experience.
This business case proposes the adoption of an AI-powered solution to automate the review and data entry of ProCare contracts. Leveraging advanced artificial intelligence technologies will streamline contract validation, drastically reduce human error, and increase operational capacity, positioning the organization for sustained growth and improved service quality.
Current State and Challenges
Contract Volume and Team Strain
•	The ProCare Contract Management Team manually processes approximately 4,500 contracts annually, with volume expected to grow by 10% each year for at least the next five years.
•	The current contract portfolio represents roughly $376 million in sales, with continual increases in contract complexity and number.
•	The team’s manual review encompasses validation of customer details, product serial numbers, purchase order accuracy, pricing, terms and conditions, signatures, and document consolidation.
•	Contracts are entered into the SAP system via CPQ (Configure Price Quote), and confirmation emails are sent to sales personnel post-entry.
Error Rates and Business Impacts
•	Manual entry error rates range between 5–10%, causing issues in contract data that impact service validation, invoicing, and customer satisfaction.
•	Human error has led to missed opportunities and downstream operational inefficiencies, particularly at critical fiscal periods (end of month, quarter, and year).
•	Business growth is exacerbating the strain on existing resources, threatening the ability to maintain service quality and meet operational SLAs.
Key Pain Points
•	Time-consuming manual validation and entry steps increase the risk of bottlenecks.
•	Suboptimal data accuracy affects billing, reporting, and customer communications.
•	Lack of scalability in current processes threatens future expansion and agility.

Problem Statement:
Problem Statement 
1. Operational Context
In warehouse or logisitics, label printers are critical to operations - they print shipping, inventory, or packaging labels required to move goods efficiently. A single printer outage can halt order creation and shipping line, delay orders and create cascading downstream impacts on fulfillment metrics. 
2. The Issue 
When the label printer outage occurs (eg print spooler crash, driver failure, network disconnection), the incident detection and resolution process is often manual, reactive and fragmented:
IT teams depend on end-user reports or delayed monitoring alerts.
Diagnosis requires manual log checks, Event Viewer review or spooler cleanup. 
Root causes may vary (corrupted print jobs, spooler service crash, driver mismatch or network issue)
Description:
Use Case Need:  
To design and deploy SRE Agent - Printer related issues that can:
Detect, Diagnose and provide recommended resolution for common printer or system outages 
Reduce manual intervention through safe automation
Provide consistent RCA documentation and feedback to the knowledge base.

Use Case: Auto Call Summary and Wrap Up Code
Comments: 12/15 --> Fer, will confirm is this case is duplicate with ID 196 Genesys AI
Description:
The Instrument Call Center Leadership is constantly looking for new ways to improve performance and the work life balance of their call center agents. 
For over seven years, Stryker call centers globally have utilized Genesys’ omnichannel software to manage customer interactions. Despite this robust technology foundation, documentation of customer calls remains largely manual, resulting in inconsistent summaries, increased after-call work, and challenges in accurately tracking commitments, especially those related to AR requests and disputes. These documentation inefficiencies contribute to operational bottlenecks and impact customer satisfaction and financial outcomes.
Genesys provides a robust suite of AI capabilities within its omnichannel software, including the Auto Call Summary and Wrap Up Code feature. Although Stryker has implemented Genesys for several years, this particular AI-driven functionality remains untapped. The Instruments division has identified significant potential in leveraging this feature to enhance operational efficiency and the customer experience.
The Auto Call Summary and Wrap Up Code feature is designed to automatically generate structured conversation summaries and assign the appropriate wrap up codes for every customer phone interaction. These summaries are then seamlessly stored within Salesforce. By utilizing AI to document calls, the process not only streamlines after-call administrative tasks but also enables agents to focus entirely on customer needs and requests during conversations.
Adopting this technology would deliver measurable value to Stryker Instruments by improving the consistency and quality of call documentation, reducing manual workload for agents, and ensuring accurate tracking of customer interactions. Ultimately, this enhancement will elevate the customer experience and drive greater efficiency within the call center, leveraging data that Genesys is already collecting.
The initiative seeks to leverage advanced AI capabilities to enhance the accuracy of customer interaction documentation, significantly reduce after-call work time, and improve the quality of accounts receivable (AR) request and dispute records. Aligning with Stryker Instruments’ strategic objectives to streamline sales requests and scale for future growth, this proposal positions Instruments as the first division in North America Commercial to adopt the feature, paving the way for future scalability to other interested divisions, including Medical, NV, ENDO, and Sports MED.

Description:
Modern software development increasingly leverages AI-assisted code generation tools (e.g., Cursor), which accelerate delivery but also introduce new risks related to code quality, maintainability, and security. Traditional static analysis tools are effective for identifying known issues but lack the context and adaptability to evaluate or remediate AI-generated code intelligently. As development velocity grows, engineering teams face growing technical debt, inconsistent code standards, and increased effort to manually review or fix low-quality code.

To address this, the integration of AI-powered analysis and automated remediation within platforms like SonarCloud is critical. AI enhances detection accuracy, prioritizes impactful issues, and even generates context-aware code fixes — allowing developers to focus on innovation rather than repetitive debugging. By combining human oversight with AI-driven insights, organizations can maintain trust, compliance, and security while accelerating their software delivery lifecycle.

Comments: Duplicate with ID 181 Auto Call Summary and Wrap Up Code
Description:
The Instrument Call Center Leadership is constantly looking for new ways to improve performance and the work life balance of their call center agents. 
For over seven years, Stryker call centers globally have utilized Genesys’ omnichannel software to manage customer interactions. Despite this robust technology foundation, documentation of customer calls remains largely manual, resulting in inconsistent summaries, increased after-call work, and challenges in accurately tracking commitments, especially those related to AR requests and disputes. These documentation inefficiencies contribute to operational bottlenecks and impact customer satisfaction and financial outcomes.
Genesys provides a robust suite of AI capabilities within its omnichannel software, including the Auto Call Summary and Wrap Up Code feature. Although Stryker has implemented Genesys for several years, this particular AI-driven functionality remains untapped. The Instruments division has identified significant potential in leveraging this feature to enhance operational efficiency and the customer experience.
The Auto Call Summary and Wrap Up Code feature is designed to automatically generate structured conversation summaries and assign the appropriate wrap up codes for every customer phone interaction. These summaries are then seamlessly stored within Salesforce. By utilizing AI to document calls, the process not only streamlines after-call administrative tasks but also enables agents to focus entirely on customer needs and requests during conversations.
Adopting this technology would deliver measurable value to Stryker Instruments by improving the consistency and quality of call documentation, reducing manual workload for agents, and ensuring accurate tracking of customer interactions. Ultimately, this enhancement will elevate the customer experience and drive greater efficiency within the call center, leveraging data that Genesys is already collecting.
The initiative seeks to leverage advanced AI capabilities to enhance the accuracy of customer interaction documentation, significantly reduce after-call work time, and improve the quality of accounts receivable (AR) request and dispute records. Aligning with Stryker Instruments’ strategic objectives to streamline sales requests and scale for future growth, this proposal positions Instruments as the first division in North America Commercial to adopt the feature, paving the way for future scalability to other interested divisions, including Medical, NV, ENDO, and Sports MED.

Problem Statement:
We currently lack end to end visibility, tracking, and intelligence across the full contract lifecycle from initial bid through negotiation, execution, performance, and renewal. Contract data is fragmented across systems including contracts, co op programs, finance, bids, and security assessments, with no centralized or queryable source of truth. As a result, we have limited insight into compliance with our own contract standards, pricing changes, co op obligations, expiration dates, and revenue protection mechanisms.

There is no systematic tracking of key contract milestones such as pricing changes, amendments, or expiration dates, no automated alerts ahead of renewals, and no ability to quickly query contract performance metrics. This creates hidden financial and compliance risk, delayed and reactive decision making, poor prioritization, misalignment across teams, slow response times, and exposure to financial leakage including missed renewals and revenue left on the table.
Description: We need a smart, flexible Contracts AI solution that provides centralized visibility and intelligence across the entire contract lifecycle. The system must enable structured tracking from bid submission through legal negotiation, execution, performance monitoring, and automated renewal alerts such as six months prior to expiration. It must allow rapid querying of contract data to assess compliance, pricing changes, co op performance, financial exposure, and overall contract health so leadership and teams can make timely, informed decisions.

Description:

Today, OTIF (On‑Time, In‑Full) analysis is largely manual, partial, and reactive, limiting our ability to consistently improve delivery performance and supply‑chain execution.

Description:
Building compensation plans in Varicent is time consuming and requires a developer to take a set of user requirements written in text and convert those into Varicent calculations (low-code set of rules and data transformations). Additionally, the developer needs some experience with the tool and a small amount of technical knowledge. Finally, building technical documentation from scratch and then trying to find documentation takes time. 

Enter Varicent's Designer Assitant,  a chat based user interface that converts natural language into Varicent Incentive calculations and vice versa. By using the requirements as written, developers can save up to 20% of build effort by having the Designer Assistant build an initial mapping off of those requirements. Also, having an interface that can assistant in the building process enables less technical resources, such as the sales compensation teams to support in the comp plan building process, eliminating resource bottlenecks. Finally, once built, the developer, or anyone who is trying to understand a compensation plan can use the designer assitant to provide technical and functional documentation, saving time.

In short, this tool will help save developer time in the initial build out of compensation plans and creation of documentation, support eliminating resource bottlenecks, and save support/business time in locating documentation to explain the system.

Description:
Employees can unlock their company match for donations made to nonprofit partners on Impact by uploading a donation receipt. Employees are not required to use this feature to access their match benefit, and any documentation they upload is their decision. To be approved, receipts must meet strict quality standards that verify the donation was made to a qualified nonprofit and that no goods or services were received in return. Currently, our team pays a $17,000 annual fee on a limited number (1,200) of manual receipt reviews conducted by Benevity for submissions above a certain threshold. The Benevity team executes our preferred standards for receipt validation. We are charged per review, and resubmissions count against our allowed amount of manual reviews. In 2025, employees submitted 2,229 match requests using this process, and 1,128 required manual review by the Benevity team. Of the manually reviewed receipts, we had a 30.5% rejection rate (344 rejections). Of those rejected, 34% (116) were due to missing name, donation date or nonprofit organization.

Employees have expressed frustration with this experience, in part because receipt requirements are documented separately in a document housed on Connect rather than being integrated into the submission process. Further, if rejected, employees must resubmit the request entirely, which increases the volume of receipts that require manual review.

Benevity has recently introduced an AI‑powered feature that pre‑screens donation receipts in real time and alerts employees to any issues directly on the submission screen. This enables employees to correct errors and re‑upload appropriate documentation before completing their submission. When an employee uploads a receipt, an A.I. validator scans the document and extracts four key pieces of information to auto-populate the form: donor name, donation amount, donation date, and nonprofit organization name. If this feature had been enabled in 2025, at least  34% (116) of the 1,128 manually reviewed receipts were resubmissions which could have been avoided.
